meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Master debugging by building a professional portfolio. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your development skills and career success.
content.answer_block: Debugging is not a hurdle; it is a skill-building opportunity. By shifting your focus from 'fixing errors' to 'architecting robust features' within a portfolio-driven workflow, you transform technical friction into production-ready code. Use Antigravity for automated architecture analysis and Claude Code to iterate faster, turning every bug into a portfolio asset.
content.intro:
The cursor blinks in an empty IDE, mocking your inability to trace a stack overflow or a silent API failure. You feel the crushing weight of 'imposter syndrome' as your project stalls. This isn't just a code error; it's a bottleneck in your growth as a software engineer. The difference between a struggling student and a senior developer isn't the absence of bugs—it's the systematic approach to solving them.

We are going to flip the narrative. Instead of viewing bugs as failures, we will treat them as the core of your portfolio. By leveraging Antigravity for structural analysis and Claude Code for rapid resolution, you will build a project that proves your technical resilience to future employers. Stop guessing, start architecting.

steps.title: Architectural Audit with Antigravity
steps.description: Before fixing, map the failure. Use Antigravity to visualize dependencies and identify where the state flow breaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase for circular dependencies and state mutation leaks causing debugging failures in my current module.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Create a minimal reproduction script. This proves you understand the bug and makes your portfolio look professional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a unit test suite that isolates the reported bug into a repeatable, failing assertion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Create a reproduction script in /tests for the current runtime error.'

steps.title: Refactor for Observability
steps.description: Add instrumentation that logs the 'why' behind the crash, not just the 'what'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the error handling middleware to provide structured, JSON-formatted logs suitable for production monitoring.

steps.title: Leverage Claude Code for Patching
steps.description: Use Claude Code's deep context awareness to apply a fix that respects your architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a design pattern change that eliminates the race condition while maintaining current interface compatibility.

steps.title: Document the 'Bug-to-Feature' Journey
steps.description: Turn this debugging session into a 'Case Study' in your README. This is what hiring managers actually want to see.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize this debugging session into a professional engineering blog post format for my GitHub README.

pro_tips: Never fix a bug without writing a regression test first; it proves your competency to future employers.
Treat your error logs as data; use them to predict future failures before they happen in production.
When using Claude Code, always provide the 'system architecture' file first to ensure contextual accuracy.
Use Antigravity's dependency graph to find 'spaghetti code' zones that are likely to break again.
Document the 'Why' of a fix in your commit messages; it creates a paper trail of high-level problem solving.
content.next_steps: Once your fix is deployed, refactor your documentation to include a 'Lessons Learned' section. This transforms a frustrating debugging session into a highlight reel for your technical portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to turn every technical hurdle into a professional achievement. Don't let bugs define your progress—let them define your expertise. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
